files:
This section contains the contents of the repository's files.
name: Build and Push Docker Image
on: workflow_dispatch: inputs: service_name: description: "Name of the service to build" required: true type: choice options: - rf-backend - crm-backend - dedup - razorpay-consumer - razorpay-producer - dsa-backend - loan-backend - loans-backend - rf-backend-hyperverge - crm-report - notification - userservice-backend
env: AWS_REGION: ap-south-1
jobs: build: name: Build Image runs-on: [self-hosted, jenkins-server] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5 - name: Set service environment variables run: | if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "rf-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/ramfincorp-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfincorp-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "razorpay-producer" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/razorpay-producer" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/function-hub" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/crm-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/node_crm" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "dedup" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/dedup" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/dedup/ramfincorp-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "dsa-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/dsa-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/DSA-Backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loan-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/loan-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/onboarding_service" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loans-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/loans-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/onboarding_service_hyperverge" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "rf-backend-hyperverge" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/rf-backend-hyperverge" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfincorp-backend-hyperverge" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-report" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/crm-report" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfin-report" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "notification" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/notification" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfincorp-notification" >> $GITHUB_ENV
elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "userservice-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=ramfincorp/userservice-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfin_userservice" >> $GITHUB_ENV fi
- name: Set image tag id: set_tag run: echo "image_tag=1.${{ github.run_number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
- name: Configure AWS credentials uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v2 with: aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }} role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
- name: Login to Amazon ECR shell: bash run: | aws ecr get-login-password --region ${{ env.AWS_REGION }} \ | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 361795871686.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com
- name: Pull latest code from backend repos shell: bash run: |
if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "rf-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master
cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/common/common-module git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master
cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/commonModules/common-module git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "dedup" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout dedup git pull origin dedup
cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/common/common-module git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "dsa-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout main-ramfin git pull origin main-ramfin elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loan-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loans-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master2 git pull origin master2 elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "razorpay-producer" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "ramfincorp-backend-hyperverge" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master2 git pull origin master2 cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/commonModules/common-module git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-report" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "notification" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "userservice-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout master git pull origin master fi
- name: Build and Push Docker image shell: bash run: | cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "rf-backend" ] \ || [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ] \ || [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "dedup" ]; then dockerfile="Dockerfile" elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "razorpay-consumer" ]; then dockerfile="Dockerfile.consumer" elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "razorpay-producer" ]; then dockerfile="Dockerfile.producer" fi docker build -t 361795871686.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }} --platform linux/amd64 -f "${dockerfile}" . docker push 361795871686.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }}
outputs: image_tag: ${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }}
deploy: needs: build name: Deploy runs-on: [self-hosted, jenkins-server] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5
- name: Deploy using deploy.sh run: | cd k8s chmod +x ./deploy.sh ./deploy.sh ${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }} ${{ needs.build.outputs.image_tag }}
name: Cleanup PR Namespaces
on: schedule: # Run every hour - cron: '0 * * * *' workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual triggering
jobs: cleanup: name: Delete old PR namespaces runs-on: [self-hosted, ramfin]
steps: - name: Clean up namespaces older than 4 hours shell: bash run: | echo "Setting kubectl context..." kubectl config use-context arn:aws:eks:ap-south-1:361795871686:cluster/rf-dev-eks-cluster
echo "Fetching namespaces containing '-pr-'..." namespaces=$(kubectl get ns -o name | awk -F'/' '/-pr-/{print $2}')
if [[ -z "${namespaces}" ]]; then echo "No PR namespaces found." exit 0 fi
CURRENT_TIME=$(date +%s)
for ns in ${namespaces}; do echo "----------------------------------------" echo "Checking namespace: $ns"
# Extract creationTimestamp creation_time_str=$(kubectl get ns "${ns}" -o jsonpath='{.metadata.creationTimestamp}')
if [[ -z "${creation_time_str}" ]]; then echo "Warning: Could not get creationTimestamp for $ns" continue fi
echo "Creation time: ${creation_time_str}"
# Try Linux GNU date creation_time=$(date -d "${creation_time_str}" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "")
# Try macOS BSD date fallback if [[ -z "${creation_time}" ]]; then creation_time=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "${creation_time_str}" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo "") fi
if [[ -z "${creation_time}" ]]; then echo "Error: Could not parse time." continue fi
# Calculate age in seconds age_seconds=$(( CURRENT_TIME - creation_time ))
# 4 hours = 4 * 60 * 60 = 14400 seconds if (( age_seconds > 14400 )); then echo "Result: Namespace ${ns} is older than 4 hours (${age_seconds} seconds)." echo "Action: Deleting namespace ${ns}..." kubectl delete namespace "${ns}" else echo "Result: Namespace ${ns} is less than 4 hours old (${age_seconds} seconds)." echo "Action: Skipping." fi done
name: Deploy to Production
on: workflow_dispatch: inputs: service_name: description: "Name of the service to deploy" required: true type: choice options: - rf-backend - crm-backend - dedup - razorpay-consumer - razorpay-producer - dsa-backend - loan-backend
service_tag: description: "Tag of the service to deploy" required: true
env: AWS_REGION: ap-south-1
jobs: build-and-deploy: name: Deploy runs-on: [self-hosted, jenkins-server] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5
- name: Verify Kubernetes Context run: | kubectl config use-context arn:aws:eks:ap-south-1:361795871686:cluster/ramfincorp if ! kubectl cluster-info > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "Error: Could not connect to the Kubernetes cluster context." exit 1 fi
- name: Deploy using deploy.sh run: | cd k8s chmod +x ./deploy.sh ./deploy.sh ${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }} ${{ github.event.inputs.service_tag }}
name: KM - Build and Push Docker Image
on: workflow_dispatch: inputs: service_name: description: "Name of the service to build" required: true type: choice options: - km-backend - crm-backend - loan-backend - loans-backend - crm-report - userservice-backend
env: AWS_REGION: ap-south-1
jobs: build: name: Build Image runs-on: [self-hosted, self-hosted-kamakshimoney] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5 - name: Set service environment variables run: | if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "km-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/km-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfincorp-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/crm-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/node_crm" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loan-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/loan-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/onboarding_service" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loans-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/loans-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/onboarding_service_KMhyperverge" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-report" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/crm-report" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfin-report" >> $GITHUB_ENV elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "userservice-backend" ]; then echo "ECR_REPOSITORY=kamakshimoney/userservice-backend" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "BASE_PATH=/home/ubuntu/api_code/ramfin_userservice" >> $GITHUB_ENV
fi - name: Set image tag id: set_tag run: echo "image_tag=1.${{ github.run_number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
- name: Configure AWS credentials uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v2 with: aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }} role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_ROLE_ARN }}
- name: Login to Amazon ECR shell: bash run: |
aws ecr get-login-password --region ${{ env.AWS_REGION }} \ | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 084828595551.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com
- name: Pull latest code from backend repos shell: bash run: |
if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "km-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshimoney-master git pull origin kamakshimoney-master
cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/common/common-module git checkout kamakshimoney_master git pull origin kamakshimoney_master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshimoney_master git pull origin kamakshimoney_master
cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}/src/commonModules/common-module git checkout kamakshimoney_master git pull origin kamakshimoney_master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loan-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshimoney_master git pull origin kamakshimoney_master elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "loans-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshimoney_master2 git pull origin kamakshimoney_master2
elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-report" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshimoney-master git pull origin kamakshimoney-master
elif [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "userservice-backend" ]; then cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }} git checkout kamakshi-master git pull origin kamakshi-master fi
- name: Build and Push Docker image shell: bash run: | cd ${{ env.BASE_PATH }}
if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "km-backend" ] || \ [ "${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }}" = "crm-backend" ]; then
dockerfile="Dockerfile" fi docker build -t 084828595551.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }} --platform linux/amd64 -f "${dockerfile}" . docker push 084828595551.dkr.ecr.${{ env.AWS_REGION }}.amazonaws.com/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }}
outputs: image_tag: ${{ steps.set_tag.outputs.image_tag }}
deploy: needs: build name: Deploy runs-on: [self-hosted, self-hosted-kamakshimoney] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5
- name: Deploy using deploy.sh run: | cd kamakshimoney-k8s chmod +x ./deploy.sh ./deploy.sh ${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }} ${{ needs.build.outputs.image_tag }}
name: Deploy to KM Production
on: workflow_dispatch: inputs: service_name: description: "Name of the service to deploy" required: true type: choice options: - rf-backend - crm-backend - dedup - razorpay-consumer - razorpay-producer - dsa-backend - loan-backend
service_tag: description: "Tag of the service to deploy" required: true
env: AWS_REGION: ap-south-1
jobs: build-and-deploy: name: Deploy runs-on: [self-hosted, self-hosted-kamakshimoney] steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v5
- name: Deploy using deploy.sh run: | cd k8s chmod +x ./deploy.sh ./deploy.sh ${{ github.event.inputs.service_name }} ${{ github.event.inputs.service_tag }}
name: "Vault Secret Manager"
on: workflow_dispatch: inputs: service: description: 'Select the Service' required: true type: choice options: - rf-backend - crm-backend - dedup - razorpay-consumer - razorpay-producer - dsa-backend - loan-backend - loans-backend - rf-backend-hyperverge - crm-report - notification - userservice-backend secret_key: description: 'Key Name (e.g. DB_PASSWORD)' required: true type: string secret_value: description: 'Key Value' required: true type: string
permissions: id-token: write contents: read
jobs: update-vault: runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Fix 1: Set the Vault Address globally for all steps in this job env: VAULT_ADDR: "https://vault.ramfincorp.com"
steps: - name: Setup Vault CLI uses: eLco/setup-vault@v1 with: vault_version: 1.15.0
- name: Authenticate with Vault uses: hashicorp/vault-action@v2 with: url: ${{ env.VAULT_ADDR }} role: github-pipeline method: jwt exportToken: true # Mandatory to provide $VAULT_TOKEN to the CLI
- name: Upsert Secret to Vault run: | # Use the logical path (no /data/ prefix) LOGICAL_PATH="secret/myapp/${{ github.event.inputs.service }}" METADATA_PATH="secret/metadata/myapp/${{ github.event.inputs.service }}"
# Fix 2: Check for existence via metadata (cleaner for KV-v2) if vault kv metadata get "$LOGICAL_PATH" > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "Secret exists at $LOGICAL_PATH. Patching key: ${{ github.event.inputs.secret_key }}" vault kv patch "$LOGICAL_PATH" \ "${{ github.event.inputs.secret_key }}=${{ github.event.inputs.secret_value }}" else echo "Secret does not exist. Creating new path: $LOGICAL_PATH" vault kv put "$LOGICAL_PATH" \ "${{ github.event.inputs.secret_key }}=${{ github.event.inputs.secret_value }}" fi
# Codeowners for this repo
* @mayank-ramfincorp @arvind-ramfincorp @sandeepsinghram999

# Staging Environment Configuration
# AWS Account setup account: "361795871686" region: "ap-south-1"
# EKS Compute Configuration node_size: "t3.medium" capacity_type: "SPOT" # or "ON_DEMAND"
apiVersion: gateway.envoyproxy.io/v1alpha1 kind: EnvoyProxy metadata: name: aws-nlb-proxy-config namespace: envoy-gateway-system spec: provider: type: Kubernetes kubernetes: envoyService: type: LoadBalancer annotations: service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-type: "external" service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-nlb-target-type: "ip" service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-scheme: "internet-facing" # ACM Certificate configuration for TLS termination service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-ssl-cert: "arn:aws:acm:ap-south-1:361795871686:certificate/99aa5845-e186-42c2-adad-1be6ee84be50" service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-ssl-ports: "443"
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: GatewayClass metadata: name: envoy-aws-gateway-class spec: controllerName: gateway.envoyproxy.io/gatewayclass-controller parametersRef: group: gateway.envoyproxy.io kind: EnvoyProxy name: aws-nlb-proxy-config namespace: envoy-gateway-system
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: Gateway metadata: name: main-gateway namespace: envoy-gateway-system spec: gatewayClassName: envoy-aws-gateway-class listeners: - name: http protocol: HTTP port: 80 # Even though you access your services via HTTPS (443), the AWS NLB terminates # the SSL. Envoy proxies generally just receive the decrypted HTTP traffic, # thus this listener is HTTP on port 80. allowedRoutes: namespaces: from: All - name: https-terminated protocol: HTTP port: 443 allowedRoutes: namespaces: from: All
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: HTTPRoute metadata: name: abc-route namespace: your-app-namespace # Replace with the namespace where the 'abc' service resides spec: parentRefs: - name: main-gateway namespace: envoy-gateway-system hostnames: - "abc.preprod.ramfincorp.com" rules: - backendRefs: - name: your-abc-kubernetes-service-name port: 8080 # The port your kubernetes service is listening on
apiVersion: v1 kind: Namespace metadata: name: test-app --- apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: name: test-nginx namespace: test-app spec: replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: test-nginx template: metadata: labels: app: test-nginx spec: containers: - name: nginx image: nginx:latest ports: - containerPort: 80 --- apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: test-nginx-svc namespace: test-app spec: selector: app: test-nginx ports: - protocol: TCP port: 80 targetPort: 80 --- apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 kind: HTTPRoute metadata: name: test-nginx-route namespace: test-app spec: parentRefs: - name: main-gateway namespace: envoy-gateway-system hostnames: - "nginx-test.ramfincorp.com" # Matches your wildcard certificate *.ramfincorp.com rules: - backendRefs: - name: test-nginx-svc port: 80
class LogColors ⋮---- INFO = '\033[94m' SUCCESS = '\033[92m' WARNING = '\033[93m' ERROR = '\033[91m' RESET = '\033[0m' ⋮---- _original_print = print ⋮---- def custom_print(*args, **kwargs) ⋮---- msg = " ".join(str(a) for a in args) lower_msg = msg.lower() ⋮---- color = LogColors.ERROR ⋮---- color = LogColors.WARNING ⋮---- color = LogColors.SUCCESS ⋮---- color = LogColors.INFO ⋮---- print = custom_print ⋮---- def load_config(env_name) ⋮---- path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "config", f"{env_name}.yaml") ⋮---- def get_clients(region) ⋮---- def create_vpc(args, config) ⋮---- ec2 = get_clients(config['region'])['ec2'] cluster_name = f"{args.env}-eks-cluster" ⋮---- # Check if VPC already exists by tag vpc_desc = ec2.describe_vpcs(Filters=[{'Name': 'tag:Name', 'Values': [f"{args.env}-eks-vpc"]}]) ⋮---- vpc = ec2.create_vpc(CidrBlock='10.0.0.0/16') vpc_id = vpc['Vpc']['VpcId'] ⋮---- # Wait for VPC waiter = ec2.get_waiter('vpc_available') ⋮---- igw = ec2.create_internet_gateway() igw_id = igw['InternetGateway']['InternetGatewayId'] ⋮---- # Dynamic subnets azs = ec2.describe_availability_zones()['AvailabilityZones'] az1 = azs[0]['ZoneName'] az2 = azs[1]['ZoneName'] ⋮---- pub_sub_1 = ec2.create_subnet(VpcId=vpc_id, CidrBlock='10.0.1.0/24', AvailabilityZone=az1) pub_sub_2 = ec2.create_subnet(VpcId=vpc_id, CidrBlock='10.0.2.0/24', AvailabilityZone=az2) priv_sub_1 = ec2.create_subnet(VpcId=vpc_id, CidrBlock='10.0.3.0/24', AvailabilityZone=az1) priv_sub_2 = ec2.create_subnet(VpcId=vpc_id, CidrBlock='10.0.4.0/24', AvailabilityZone=az2) ⋮---- pub_ids = [pub_sub_1['Subnet']['SubnetId'], pub_sub_2['Subnet']['SubnetId']] priv_ids = [priv_sub_1['Subnet']['SubnetId'], priv_sub_2['Subnet']['SubnetId']] ⋮---- # # AWS EKS Automation Scripts
This directory contains pure Python scripts (powered by `boto3`) to reliably deploy and safely destroy Amazon EKS clusters and their associated dependencies (IAM Roles, Managed Node Groups) across different environments.
## Pre-requisites 1. **Python >= 3.9** 2. Install the necessary Python dependencies: ```bash pip install -r requirements.txt ``` 3. Establish your **AWS Credentials**. You can do this securely by running `aws configure` in your terminal or setting environment variables (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`).
## Configuration Before running any script, you must populate the target environment's YAML config: - `config/pre-prod.yaml` - `config/staging.yaml`
---
## 1. Deploying EKS (`deploy_eks.py`)
This script provisions the IAM policies/roles, EKS Cluster control plane, and the Managed Node Group. It automatically tracks progress and blocks until resources are fully `ACTIVE`.
### Usage ```bash python deploy_eks.py ```
### Arguments * `` (Required): The target configuration environment. Options: `pre-prod` or `staging`. * `` (Required): The specific step to execute. * `create-vpc` : Generates a brand new VPC specifically for this environment, configured with public/private subnets, an IGW, and a NAT Gateway. Validates by resource tags. * `create-roles` : Creates the `EksClusterRole` and `EksNodeRole` IAM roles and attaches the correct AWS managed policies. * `create-cluster` : Initiates the creation of the EKS Cluster using the target config. * `wait-cluster` : Blocks and waits until the EKS Cluster status changes to `ACTIVE`. * `deploy-metrics` : Deploys the Kubernetes Metrics Server into the newly created cluster. * `deploy-aws-lbc` : Installs the AWS Load Balancer Controller via Helm into the cluster. * `create-nodegroup` : Initiates the creation of the EC2 Managed Node Group. * `wait-nodegroup` : Blocks and waits until the Node Group status changes to `ACTIVE`. * `create-schedule` : Creates auto-scaling schedules to automatically scale down the Node Group at night and scale up in the morning. * `create-admin-user` : Provisions an IAM user (`-eks-admin`), maps it to the EKS cluster with admin privileges, and securely returns the AWS credentials. * **`deploy-all`** : Runs all of the above deploy commands sequentially in the correct order.
**Example deploy:** ```bash python deploy_eks.py pre-prod deploy-all ```
---
## 2. Destroying EKS (`destroy_eks.py`)
This script safely tears down the EKS ecosystem in the required reverse order. **You will be securely prompted to confirm (`yes`/`no`) before any actual deletion occurs.**
### Usage ```bash python destroy_eks.py ```
### Arguments * `` (Required): The target configuration environment. Options: `pre-prod` or `staging`. * `` (Required): The specific step to execute. * `delete-nodegroup` : Deletes the Managed Node Group and waits until it is fully terminated from AWS. * `delete-cluster` : Deletes the core EKS Cluster and waits until it is fully terminated. * `delete-roles` : Detaches all AWS managed policies and completely deletes the associated IAM roles. * `delete-vpc` : Destroys the created VPC, cleans up its NAT gateways, EIPs, Internet Gateways, Subnets, and Route Tables. * `delete-admin-user` : Deletes the created EKS admin IAM user, dropping all associated inline policies and access keys. * **`destroy-all`** : Runs all of the above deletion commands sequentially in the correct teardown order.
**Example destroy:** ```bash python destroy_eks.py staging destroy-all ```
---
## 3. Infrastructure
### Installing Envoy Gateway
To install the Gateway API CRDs and Envoy Gateway into the `envoy-gateway-system` namespace, run the following official Helm command:
```bash helm install eg oci://docker.io/envoyproxy/gateway-helm --version v1.7.1 -n envoy-gateway-system --create-namespace ```
Wait until the Envoy Gateway deployment is available:
```bash kubectl wait --timeout=5m -n envoy-gateway-system deployment/envoy-gateway --for=condition=Available ```
### Configuring Envoy To ensure Envoy loads the load balancer properly and can route traffic correctly, you must apply the following core configurations to the cluster after installation: 1. `aws-proxy` 2. `gateway-class` 3. `gateway`
Make sure these base configuration files are correctly applied using `kubectl apply -f` in your infrastructure environment before attempting to deploy your applications.
### 4. Updating Route 53 (DNS Mapping) When you successfully apply the `gateway` configuration in the previous step, Envoy Gateway provisions an AWS Load Balancer (typically an NLB) and AWS assigns it a dynamic DNS address automatically.
In order for your domain traffic to route to the cluster correctly, you must: 1. **Retrieve the Load Balancer URL:** Run `kubectl get gateway -n ` and extract the load balancer hostname listed under `ADDRESS`. 2. **Update Route 53:** Log into your AWS Route 53 Console and create or update an **Alias** record (e.g., `*.preprod.ramfincorp.com`) mapping directly to the new Load Balancer DNS name retrieved above. Ensure your Alias targets an *Application and Classic Load Balancer* or *Network Load Balancer*.

name: userservice-backend namespace: userservice-backend image: repository: 361795871686.dkr.ecr.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/ramfincorp/userservice-backend tag: "1.0" pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
service: name: userservice-backend-svc type: ClusterIP port: 8080
probe: liveness: endpoint: "/health" initialDelaySeconds: 120 periodSeconds: 30
resources: requests: memory: "1024Mi" cpu: "1000m" limits: memory: "1500Mi" cpu: "1000m"
#!/usr/bin/env bash
serviceName="${1}" imageTag="${2}" if [ -z "${serviceName}" ] || [ -z "${imageTag}" ]; then echo "Usage: $0 " exit 1 fi namespace="${serviceName}"
if [ "${serviceName}" == "razorpay-producer" ]; then namespace="rf-backend" fi echo "Deploying ${serviceName} with image tag ${imageTag} in namespace: ${namespace}"
kubectl create namespace "${namespace}" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - case "${serviceName}" in "loan-backend"|"crm-backend"|"rf-backend"|"dsa-backend") echo "Creating/Updating secret ${serviceName}-env in namespace: ${namespace}" kubectl create secret generic "${serviceName}-env" \ --from-file=.env=/home/ubuntu/api_code/env/"${serviceName}"-prod-env \ --namespace "${namespace}" \ --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - ;; esac helm upgrade --install \ "${serviceName}" "./${serviceName}" \ --set image.tag="${imageTag}" \ --namespace "${namespace}" \ --create-namespace \ -f ./"${serviceName}"/values.yaml
loki:$apr1$PgQHQnBZ$v3emtbjPD5AbbCNiy6lTb.
{ "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Sid": "LokiStorage", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "s3:ListBucket", "s3:PutObject", "s3:GetObject", "s3:DeleteObject" ], "Resource": [ "arn:aws:s3:::prod-k8s-loki-chunks", "arn:aws:s3:::prod-k8s-loki-chunks/*", "arn:aws:s3:::prod-k8s-loki-ruler", "arn:aws:s3:::prod-k8s-loki-ruler/*" ] } ] }
loki: auth_enabled: false schemaConfig: configs: - from: "2024-04-01" store: tsdb object_store: s3 schema: v13 index: prefix: loki_index_ period: 24h storage_config: aws: region: ap-south-1 # for example, eu-west-2 bucketnames: prod-k8s-loki-chunks # Your actual S3 bucket name, for example, loki-aws-dev-chunks s3forcepathstyle: false ingester: chunk_encoding: snappy pattern_ingester: enabled: true limits_config: allow_structured_metadata: true volume_enabled: true retention_period: 672h # 28 days retention compactor: retention_enabled: true delete_request_store: s3 ruler: enable_api: true storage: type: s3 s3: region: ap-south-1 # for example, eu-west-2 bucketnames: prod-k8s-loki-ruler # Your actual S3 bucket name, for example, loki-aws-dev-ruler s3forcepathstyle: false alertmanager_url: http://prom:9093 # The URL of the Alertmanager to send alerts (Prometheus, Mimir, etc.)
querier: max_concurrent: 4
storage: type: s3 bucketNames: chunks: "prod-k8s-loki-chunks" # Your actual S3 bucket name (loki-aws-dev-chunks) ruler: "prod-k8s-loki-ruler" # Your actual S3 bucket name (loki-aws-dev-ruler) # admin: "" # Your actual S3 bucket name (loki-aws-dev-admin) - GEL customers only s3: region: ap-south-1 # eu-west-2 #insecure: false # s3forcepathstyle: false
serviceAccount: create: true annotations: "eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn": "arn:aws:iam::361795871686:role/LokiServiceAccountRole" # The service role you created
deploymentMode: Distributed
ingester: replicas: 2 zoneAwareReplication: enabled: false
querier: replicas: 1 maxUnavailable: 2
queryFrontend: replicas: 1 maxUnavailable: 1
queryScheduler: replicas: 1
distributor: replicas: 1 maxUnavailable: 2 compactor: replicas: 1
indexGateway: replicas: 1 maxUnavailable: 1
ruler: replicas: 1 maxUnavailable: 1
# This exposes the Loki gateway so it can be written to and queried externaly gateway: service: type: LoadBalancer basicAuth: enabled: false
# Since we are using basic auth, we need to pass the username and password to the canary lokiCanary: extraArgs: - -pass=$(LOKI_PASS) - -user=$(LOKI_USER) extraEnv: - name: LOKI_PASS valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: canary-basic-auth key: password - name: LOKI_USER valueFrom: secretKeyRef: name: canary-basic-auth key: username
# Enable minio for storage minio: enabled: false
backend: replicas: 0 read: replicas: 0 write: replicas: 0
singleBinary: replicas: 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
serviceName="${1}" imageTag="${2}" if [ -z "${serviceName}" ] || [ -z "${imageTag}" ]; then echo "Usage: $0 " exit 1 fi namespace="${serviceName}"
if [ "${serviceName}" == "razorpay-producer" ]; then namespace="rf-backend" fi echo "Deploying ${serviceName} with image tag ${imageTag} in namespace: ${namespace}" helm upgrade --install \ "${serviceName}" "./${serviceName}" \ --set image.tag="${imageTag}" \ --namespace "${namespace}" \ --create-namespace \ -f ./"${serviceName}"/values.yaml
FROM python:3.14.3-trixie
# Install system dependencies RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \ curl \ unzip \ nodejs \ npm \ git \ jq \ && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
# Install AWS CLI RUN curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o "awscliv2.zip" \ && unzip awscliv2.zip \ && ./aws/install \ && rm -rf aws awscliv2.zip
# Install kubectl RUN curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl" \ && chmod +x kubectl \ && mv kubectl /usr/local/bin/
# Install Helm RUN curl -fsSL -o get_helm.sh https://raw.githubusercontent.com/helm/helm/main/scripts/get-helm-3 \ && chmod 700 get_helm.sh \ && ./get_helm.sh \ && rm get_helm.sh
# Install AWS CDK CLI RUN npm install -g aws-cdk
# Set working directory (usually mapped to the repository root in GitHub Actions or Local mounts) WORKDIR /workspace
# Default command CMD ["/bin/bash"]
# Auto-generated by CodeGraphContext # Default ignore patterns for binary/media files # Add your own patterns below
node_modules/ venv/ .venv/ env/ .env/ dist/ build/ target/ out/ .git/ __pycache__/ *.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.gif *.svg *.mp4 *.mp3 *.zip *.tar *.gz
**/.env
#Install Grafan using S3 storage
kubectl create ns monitoring
kubectl create secret generic loki-s3-secret -n monitoring --from-literal=AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID= --from-literal=AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=
mkdir monitoring
vim monitoring/values.yaml
#add this on yaml_file loki: enabled: true
# Mount AWS creds from secret extraEnvFrom: - secretRef: name: loki-s3-secret
config: schema_config: configs: - from: 2020-10-24 store: boltdb-shipper object_store: s3 schema: v12 index: prefix: index_ period: 24h
storage_config: aws: s3: prod-k8s-loki-chunks # S3 bucket for chunks region: ap-south-1 boltdb_shipper: shared_store: s3 active_index_directory: /tmp/loki/index cache_location: /tmp/loki/cache
ruler: storage: type: s3 s3: bucketnames: prod-k8s-loki-ruler region: ap-south-1
grafana: enabled: true adminPassword: "Admin@1231233455"
#create using helm
helm repo update
helm upgrade --install loki grafana/loki-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace -f monitoring/values.yaml
kubectl logs loki-0 -n monitoring --previous
#to check storage location kubectl exec -n monitoring -it loki-0 -- ls -l /tmp/loki/index
kubectl exec -n monitoring -it loki-0 -- ls -l /tmp/loki/cache
aws s3 ls s3://prod-k8s-loki-chunks/ --region ap-south-1
# using nodePort access with Local IP kubectl patch svc loki-grafana -n monitoring -p '{"spec": {"type": "NodePort"}}'
kubectl get svc -n monitoring loki-grafana
kubectl get pods -n monitoring -o wide curl http://10.1.21.0:30849/
# devOpsStack DevOps tools and pipelines for ramfincorp ## DevOpsStack Helm Charts
This repository organizes Kubernetes Helm charts for deploying various services. Each service has its own folder under `k8s/`, containing its Helm chart and related resources.
## Structure
``` k8s/ deploy.sh # Script to deploy any Helm chart in this repo / # Each service has its own folder Chart.yaml # Helm chart definition values.yaml # Default values for the chart templates/ # Kubernetes manifests (deployment, service, etc.) common-resources/ # Shared resources (namespace, ingress class, etc.) externalServices.yaml ingressClass.yaml namespace.yaml .github/ workflows/ # GitHub Actions workflows for CI/CD ```
## Adding a New Helm Chart 1. Create a new folder under `k8s/` for your service (e.g., `my-service/`). 2. Add your Helm chart files (`Chart.yaml`, `values.yaml`, `templates/`). 3. Update `deploy.sh` to support deploying your new chart. 4. (Optional) Add or update workflows in `.github/workflows` for automated deployment.
## Deployment - Use `k8s/deploy.sh` to deploy any chart. The script will prompt for the chart/service name and handle deployment using Helm. - Common resources are managed in `k8s/common-resources/` and should be applied before deploying individual charts if needed.
``` cd k8s ./deploy.sh rf-backend
# or for crm ./deploy.sh crm-backend
```
## CI/CD - Workflows in `.github/workflows` automate deployment and other tasks. Update or add workflows as needed for new charts.
--- For more details, see individual chart folders and scripts.
## Infra Setup
### EKS Set the EKS Auto Mode for easier setup from UI
### Monitoring
- Commands to execute to setup prometheus and grafana
``` helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack -n monitoring --create-namespace ```
- To access the grafana dashboard:
``` k port-forward svc/prometheus-grafana 9091:80 ``` Log into grafana dashboard with these credentials: admin/prom-operator
